text: Standing in this thoughtfully designed space, I am struck by the quiet, almost clinical order of the environment. The glass and light lend a sense of transparency and openness, yet I also feel like a watcher on the threshold of meaning. Here, on the edge of a room that vibrates with potential knowledge, I am both present and slightly disoriented, my reflection almost merging with the scene. It is an invitation to consider boundaries—the tangible and the abstract—between me and the world around me.
